16 March 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Next Flashpoint In Iran War? The Bab Al-Mandab Strait Off Yemen's Coast RFE/RL 16 Mar 2026 -- Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to a massive US-Israel bombing campaign has wreaked havoc on world energy markets and sent oil prices soaring.

  • US Used 10% of Ready-to-Fire Tomahawk Arsenal in First 3 Days of Iran Operation - Reports Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- The US Navy launched about 10% of its ready-to-fire Tomahawk missile arsenal during the first three days of the military operation against Iran, The National Interest magazine reported on Monday.
  • US Reportedly Close To Announcing Hormuz Escort Coalition; Iraq Feels War Effects RFE/RL 16 Mar 2026 -- The United States is close to announcing a coalition to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the Wall Street Journal reported, while Iran warned that it is not close to giving up and neighboring Iraq continued to feel the spillover effects of the war.

  • Attack on Kharg oil facilities will trigger strikes on origin country's energy sites: Armed Forces Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Iran's armed forces have issued a warning that any aggression against the strategic Kharg Island oil terminal will be met with the complete destruction of oil and gas facilities in the country from which the attack originates.
  • 'Will hit enemy where it least expects': Iran Navy chief vows to avenge blood of Dena martyrs Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Iranian Navy, Rear Admiral Shahram Irani, issued a warning to the United States and Israel on Wednesday, vowing that the enemy will face deadly retaliation for the massacre of Iranian Navy officers.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 16 Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Iranian armed forces and resistance groups across the region continue to carry out retaliatory military operations against the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • US attack on Dena destroyer violation of international law: Iran's judiciary chief Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The head of Iran's judiciary has denounced the US Navy's deadly attack on the Dena destroyer off the coast of Sri Lanka as a violation of all international conventions, particularly those governing international maritime law.
  • Israel admits over 120 injured in retaliatory Iranian strikes in past 24 hours Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Israel's health ministry says that more than 120 settlers have been injured in retaliatory Iranian attacks over the past 24 hours.
  • IRGC orders evacuation of US-linked industrial facilities in West Asia region Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has once again urged workers to evacuate US-affiliated industrial facilities in West Asia ahead of Iran's retaliatory attacks.
  • Iran says over 61,000 civilian buildings damaged in US-Israeli attacks Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian government spokeswoman says more than 61,000 civilian buildings have been damaged in the illegal US-Israeli aggression against the country.
  • Iran's Army launches retaliatory drone strikes on Israeli arms companies Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Army of the Islamic Republic of Iran has launched retaliatory drone strikes on strategic centers belonging to Israeli weapons manufacturers, including Rafael and Industries Aircraft Israel (IAI).
  • No oil tankers sailing through Strait of Hormuz: Report Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Data from a major international tanker‑tracking service shows that no oil tankers are currently sailing through the Strait of Hormuz, the key waterway in the Persian Gulf responsible for about one‑fifth of global oil demand which has been under full control of the Iranian armed forces since early March, days after the start of the US-Israeli aggression on the country.
  • Fresh wave of IRGC missile strikes hit Israeli, US targets Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched the 55th wave of its retaliatory strikes as part of Operation True Promise 4, targeting Israeli positions in the occupied territories and US interests at regional military bases in retaliation for the ongoing aggression against Iran.
  • Attack on Kharg Island would transform global energy equation, IRGC Navy chief warns Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Chief Commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri has warned that any attack on Iran's Kharg Island would fundamentally alter global energy markets.
  • Iran to Fight US Until Trump Realizes Conflict With Iran Was Mistake - Top Diplomat Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- Iran will continue to resist attack by the US armed forces until US President Donald Trump realizes that the conflict he initiated was a mistake and must never be repeated, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Monday.
  • Iran declares support centers for USS Gerald R. Ford legitimate targets Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a warning that all logistical and service centers supporting the US aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford in the Red Sea are now considered legitimate targets for Iranian armed forces, as the warship takes refuge at Saudi Arabia's Jeddah port.

  • Trump Says US Doesn't Know If Iran Leader Is Alive, Calls For Support On Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 16 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump questioned whether Iran's new supreme leader is alive and pressed Washington's call for help securing shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, which Tehran has effectively shut down with attacks on vessels and threats of more.
  • Coalition To Protect Shipping In Allies' Own Interest, GOP Strategist Says RFE/RL 16 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has called on allies to help secure the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, urging partners to send naval forces to keep global energy shipments flowing through the waterway amid the Iran conflict.
  • Trump Calls on European, Arab Allies to 'Do More' to Open Strait of Hormuz - White House Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump is trying to encourage European allies and partners in the Arab world to join the US in its effort to "open" the Strait of Hormuz, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Monday.
  • Trump Warns NATO Faces 'Very Bad Future' if Allies Don't Help in Hormuz — Reports Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump warned that NATO could face a "very bad" future if US allies fail to assist Washington in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, according to an interview with The Financial Times.
  • US Seeking Allies to 'Police' the Strait of Hormuz — Trump Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- President Donald Trump said the United States is discussing cooperation with other countries on "policing" the Strait of Hormuz, speaking aboard Air Force One.
  • Trump claims Iran uses AI as 'disinformation weapon' to misrepresent its wartime success Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has claimed without providing any evidence that Iran uses artificial intelligence (AI) to spread disinformation about its wartime success.

  • Iran FM denies contact with US envoy, says rumors geared to mislead oil traders Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has issued a statement categorically denying recent US media reports claiming he had been in contact with American envoy Steve Witkoff, stating that his last communication with the envoy occurred before Washington's decision to "kill diplomacy" through another illegal military attack on Iran.
  • Larijani mocks Trump's claims on Iran rallies being AI, draws parallel to Pahlavi Era Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, Ali Larijani, has rejected recent remarks by Donald Trump, who dismissed the recent massive anti-US and anti-Israel demonstrations across Iran as being generated by artificial intelligence, suggesting that the American president doesn't want to see the reality of situation.
  • Iranians will never capitulate to bullying, resist brutal aggression: Foreign Ministry spokesman Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian people will never yield to bullying and will continue to resist the brutal aggression against their homeland by the United States and Israel, says the Foreign Ministry spokesman.
  • 'US not loyal to you, Israel your enemy', Iran's top security official warns Muslim nations Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Iran's top security official has told Islamic countries that the United States has proven it is not a reliable partner for them and that the Israeli regime is their enemy.
  • Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei endorses officials appointed by martyred Leader Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei has endorsed officials appointed by his martyred predecessor, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, directing them to remain in their posts.
  • Araghchi: Neighbors should 'promptly' clarify stance on 'slaughter' of Iranians Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran's neighboring countries should "promptly" clarify their position regarding their role in the "slaughter" of Iranian civilians by the Israeli regime and the United States.
  • 'Iran will continue fighting until no such aggression is ever imposed again' Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei says the Islamic Republic will continue fighting the war with the United States and Israel until it makes certain no such aggression will ever be imposed on the country again.
  • Iran after permanent end to war, will soon celebrate victory: Araghchi Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran seeks a permanent cessation of the US-Israeli war on its soil, saying the unprovoked aggression should end in a way that would prevent enemies from ever repeating such atrocities.
  • Iran's supreme leader reiterates demand for compensation from 'enemy' Global Times 16 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei on Sunday night reiterated his country's demand for compensation from its "enemy."

  • China urges de-escalation as US seeks help in securing Hormuz passage; US tries to drag in others to turn issue into a multilateral one: expert Global Times 16 Mar 2026 -- As Washington, facing the likelihood of being mired in longer conflict in Iran war, urged other countries, including China, to help assist in passage of the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, most US allies have kept distant from the request. On Monday, China's foreign ministry spokesperson took a stream of questions over the matter.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Six Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 16 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Seven Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 16 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Discusses Current Developments with Pakistani Counterpart SPA 16 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah held a phone call today with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan Ishaq Dar
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Eight Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 16 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Discusses Current Developments with Bahraini Counterpart SPA 16 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call today from Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Bahrain Dr. Abdullatif bin Rashid Al Zayani
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Al-Kharj Governorate SPA 16 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: 11 Drones Destroyed in Al-Kharj Governorate SPA 16 Mar 2026

  • PM remarks: 16 March 2026 Gov.UK 16 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister's remarks at a press conference in Downing Street.
  • UK 'will not be drawn into wider war,' says Starmer Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 16 Mar 2026 -- The UK will not be drawn into a wider war in the Middle East, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday, as tensions in the region continue, Anadolu Agency reported.
  • UK Resists Sending Warships to Hormuz Mission Sought by Trump — Reports Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- Britain is not prepared to send warships to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, despite US President Donald Trump's call for allies to join a naval effort, The Telegraph reported.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: we are ready to discuss the situation in the Strait of Hormuz with the United States Estonia MFA 16 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna said today, 16 March, that if the United States wishes to discuss the situation in the Strait of Hormuz with its allies, Estonia is ready to do so.
  • During a Telephone Call with HE GCCSG, Foreign Minister of Ukraine Reaffirms Ukraine's Stance and Support for the GCC States against Treacherous Iranian Attacks on their Territories Gulf Cooperation Council 16 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG Receives HE the Egyptian Minister of Foreign Affairs and Emigration to Discuss Brutal Iranian Aggressions Against GCC States Gulf Cooperation Council 16 Mar 2026
  • Japan-United Arab Emirates Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 16 Mar 2026
  • Japan-Saudi Arabia Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 16 Mar 2026
  • Statement by the Russian Foreign Ministry regarding the escalation of the crisis in the Middle East Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Mar 2026
  • US and Israel realize Iran aggression 'a miscalculation': Russia's Lavrov Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Russia's top diplomat says the United States and Israel have come to the realization that their military aggression against Iran was a miscalculation.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Joint Interactive Dialogue on Iran UK FCDO 16 Mar 2026 -- UK Statement for the Joint Interactive Dialogue with the Special Rapporteur on the Islamic Republic of Iran and the Fact-Finding Mission on Iran. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • U.S. Army exchange officers build trust, strength within NATO Army News 16 Mar 2026 -- Far from the familiar formations of the U.S. Army, a select group of American Soldiers is deeply embedded within the military forces of their European allies. Each year, U.S. Army NATO brings these exchange officers together for a crucial training and leader development event, ensuring they remain ready and connected while serving in isolated assignments across seven countries.
  • Flagship of U.S. 7th Fleet arrives in Manila, Philippines U.S. 7th Fleet 16 Mar 2026 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) arrived in Manila, Philippines for a scheduled port visit, March 16, 2026. Blue Ridge's last visit to Manila was in 2024.
  • NIWC Pacific AI Program to Guide Next-Generation Chip Development US Navy 16 Mar 2026 -- A Naval Information Warfare Center (NIWC) Pacific artificial intelligence program is poised to help guide the development of a new generation of American-made microchips, following a high-level summit hosted by the University of Arizona on Feb. 18.
  • PCU Harvey C. Barnum Jr. (DDG 124) Sailors Arrive in Boston for Scheduled Port Visit US Navy 16 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Navy Sailors assigned to the Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer Pre-Commissioning Unit (PCU) Harvey C. Barnum Jr. (DDG 124) arrived in Boston, Massachusetts, March 13 for a scheduled port visit.
  • Navy Reshapes Warfighting Acquisition System US Navy Press Release 16 Mar 2026 -- The Department of the Navy (DON) today announced the establishment of five Portfolio Acquisition Executive organizations: PAE Industrial Operations, PAE Marine Corps, PAE Maritime, PAE Strategic Systems Programs and PAE Undersea. With these directives, the Navy is fully engaged in making the PAE model the new operational standard for the acquisition enterprise, injecting urgency and a ruthless focus on accelerated delivery.
  • CORE26 | German-French Air Squadron Transports U.S. Marine Rifle Company for First Time in NATO History USMC News 16 Mar 2026 -- In a historic demonstration of NATO's expanding capabilities, a rifle company of U.S. Marines were airlifted in the Arctic Circle by a unique, jointly operated, French and German air transport squadron, March 11, marking the first time the squadron has ever transported United States Marines.

  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on March 9 - 15, 2026 Lithuania MND 16 Mar 2026
  • NATO Allies and Indo-Pacific Partners deepen cyber defence cooperation NATO 16 Mar 2026 -- On Monday (16 March), the Cyber Champions Summit was held in Prague, Czechia. NATO Allies, along with partners Australia, Japan, New Zealand and the Republic of Korea agreed to further develop cyber defence cooperation, including through enhanced expert level exchange at the political, military and technical levels.
  • Joint statement on Cold Response 26 and NATO's Forward Land Forces in Finland by the Ministers of Defence of Finland, Sweden and Norway Government Offices of Sweden 16 Mar 2026 -- NATO's Forward Land Forces Finland forms the 9th FLF in NATO and is an important part of NATO's enhanced deterrence and defence posture in the High North.
  • Joint statement on Cold Response 26 and NATO's Forward Land Forces in Finland by the Ministers of Defence of Finland, Sweden and Norway Finnish MoD 16 Mar 2026 -- Joint statement on Cold Response 26 and NATO's Forward Land Forces in Finland by the Ministers of Defence of Finland, Sweden and Norway on 16 March 2026

  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's address to participants in the Moscow Non-Proliferation Conference Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Mar 2026
  • Lavrov Says Fate of Nuclear Nonproliferation Regime of Serious Concern Sputnik 16 Mar 2026 -- The fate of the nuclear nonproliferation regime is of serious concern, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Monday.

  • NCSIST plans low-cost munitions to counter China's potential attack CNA 16 Mar 2026 -- Taiwan's main weapons research and development unit is planning to develop low-cost munitions to counter China's potential use of similar weapons in a conflict that could deplete Taiwan's stock of air defense missiles.

  • Popular domain takes up more space Australia DOD 16 Mar 2026 -- Five years ago, the space component at Exercise Cobra Gold amounted to just two personnel on a trestle table in the corner of a room.
  • Exercise Sea Dragon 26 Commences in Guam Australia DOD 16 Mar 2026 -- The Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) has deployed a P-8A Poseidon aircraft and 50 aviators to Andersen Air Force Base in Guam for Exercise Sea Dragon 2026.
  • Six navies align for Exercise Kakadu Australia DOD 16 Mar 2026 -- The command teams of warships from six nations came together at HMAS Coonawarra, Darwin, for the first harbour briefings in support of Exercise Kakadu.

  • Japan-U.S. Defense Ministerial Telephone Conference (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 16 Mar 2026
  • Japan-Italy Defense Ministerial Video Conference (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 16 Mar 2026

  • Minister McGuinty Announces Strategic Investments in Sovereign Space Launch Canada 16 Mar 2026 -- Amid a more complex and unpredictable security environment, Canada is taking decisive actions to strengthen its security and sovereignty and to reinforce the resilience and long-term strength of the country.

  • Long-term defence commitment in Scotland helps secure jobs and billions in export deals UK MOD 16 Mar 2026 -- Increased defence investment and dedication to Scottish defence industry directly supported £1.1bn in inward investment with defence firm Thales, securing and sustaining over 500 jobs.
  • UK and Germany meet to advance deep precision strike missile programme to boost national security UK MOD 16 Mar 2026 -- Minister for Defence Readiness and Industry, Luke Pollard, met his German counterpart in Berlin to discuss progress on jointly developing Deep Precision Strike missiles.

  • Resolution 1540 remains as important to international security today as when it was negotiated over twenty years ago: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 16 Mar 2026 -- Statement by Jennifer MacNaughtan, UK Minister Counsellor, at the Security Council meeting on the 1540 Committee.
  • Security Council Delegates Stress Importance of Resolution 1540 (2004) as Risks of Weapons of Mass Destruction Proliferation Rise Globally UN Security Council 16 Mar 2026 -- The Security Council was briefed today on the work of its subsidiary body tasked with monitoring and supporting efforts to prevent the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction to non-State actors, as speakers underscored the importance of its work in a tumultuous time for global security.

  • Sejjil-2: Iran's two-stage, solid-fuel ballistic missile that has become Israel's nightmare Press TV 16 Mar 2026

  • Defence Ministers of Finland, Sweden and Norway at Cold Response 26: Implementation of FLF Finland progressing rapidly Finnish MoD 16 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen hosted the countries participating in FLF Finland at the Cold Response 26 exercise at Rovajärvi on 16 March 2026.

  • Latvia and Germany strengthen defense cooperation by signing a memorandum on "IRIS-T" system maintenance in the Baltics Latvia MOD 16 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, March 16, Minister of Defence Andris Sprūds visited Berlin on a working trip, where he met with the Federal Minister of Defence of the Federal Republic of Germany, Boris Pistorius. During the visit, the minister also attended the signing ceremony of a memorandum of understanding between the German defence industry company "Diehl Defence" and the Latvian company "Ripo Remonta Centrs".

  • Gitanas Nausėda discussed proposals on nuclear deterrence with the Latvian leader President of the Republic of Lithuania 16 Mar 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda spoke by telephone with Latvian President Edgars Rinkēvičs on Monday.

  • Disarmament: Council adopts decision supporting African capacities towards a mine-free continent EC/CEU 16 Mar 2026 -- The Council today adopted a decision supporting efforts to strengthen African capacities for mine action, contributing to the objective of a mine-free Africa.

  • No planned reduction in PH participation in joint exercises Philippine News Agency 16 Mar 2026 -- A ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official on Monday said there is still no planned reduction of its joint exercises with other countries amid steady oil price increases.

  • FM Sa'ar speaks with the Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister of Malta, Ian Borg Israel MFA 16 Mar 2026 -- FM Sa'ar briefed Deputy PM and FM Borg on the developments in the war against the Iranian terror regime.

  • Hezbollah hits Israeli targets as regime forces invade southern Lebanon Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Lebanese resistance movement Hezbollah says it has successfully destroyed Israeli targets in the heart of the occupied territories as Israel launches a ground invasion of southern Lebanon.
  • Hezbollah attacks Israeli positions in northern occupied territories Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Lebanese resistance movement Hezbollah says its fighters have attacked the Israeli positions in the northern parts of the occupied territories.

  • Statement by the leaders of Canada, France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom on the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah. Élysée - President of France 16 Mar 2026
  • Joint statement on the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah: 16 March 2026 UK FCDO 16 Mar 2026 -- Statement by the leaders of Canada, France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom on the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.
  • Lebanon - Attack on UNIFIL (16 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 16 Mar 2026 -- France strongly condemns the shots fired several times at soldiers of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) yesterday.
  • Sweden provides humanitarian support to Lebanon Government Offices of Sweden 16 Mar 2026 -- Sweden is announcing a new SEK 65 million humanitarian aid package for Lebanon due to the ongoing military escalation between Israel and Hezbollah, which has caused a rapid deterioration in the humanitarian situation in the country.
  • No: 49, 16 March 2026, Regarding Israel's Ground Offensive in Lebanon Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Mar 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Middle East, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine, Sudan UNOCHA 16 Mar 2026

  • Middle East war's 'spiral of conflict' drives mounting civilian toll UN News 16 Mar 2026 -- The widening war in the Middle East and its growing impact on civilians came under scrutiny at the UN in Geneva on Monday, as independent experts briefing the Human Rights Council warned of escalating violence following the onset of Israeli and US strikes on Iran and counterstrikes by Tehran and allied groups.
  • WHO releases more than $2 million in emergency funds to Lebanon, Iraq and Syria Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 16 Mar 2026 -- The World Health Organisation said on March 15 it had released US$2 million (S$2.6 million) from its Contingency Fund for Emergencies (CFE) to support the health response in Lebanon, Iraq and Syria amidst the ongoing crisis in the Middle East, according to Reuters.
  • SDC steps up humanitarian support for people in Lebanon, Syria and Iran Federal Council - Swiss Government 16 Mar 2026 -- The war in the Middle East is having a serious impact on the populations of Lebanon, Syria and Iran. In view of this situation, the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) has set aside a humanitarian support package of CHF 6.5 million, which will focus on Lebanon and includes a regional component for Syria. The SDC will also release emergency aid for Iran.
  • EU announces €458 million in humanitarian aid for the war-torn Middle East European Commission 16 Mar 2026 -- The European Commission is confirming €450 million in humanitarian aid for Palestine, Lebanon, Syria, Jordan and Egypt in 2026. With major donors withdrawing from the region and international humanitarian law under unprecedented strain, the EU is sustaining life-saving assistance to millions of people.

  • War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief UN News 16 Mar 2026 -- The disruption of global energy supplies is being felt worldwide, the UN's top climate change official warned on Monday, as conflict in the Middle East drives oil and gas prices sharply higher - echoing the market turmoil triggered by the war in Ukraine.

  • DFA asserts PH sovereignty over Bajo de Masinloc, Kalayaan Islands Philippine News Agency 16 Mar 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) reiterated on Monday the Philippines' longstanding sovereignty over Bajo de Masinloc and Kalayaan Island Group.
  • Statement of the DFA Spokesperson for Maritime Affairs on Sovereignty of the Philippines over Bajo De Masinloc Philippines DFA 16 Mar 2026 -- In response to the Chinese Embassy's erroneous and misleading statement issued on Saturday, 14 March 2026, the Department of Foreign Affairs firmly underscores the Philippines' indivisible, incontrovertible and longstanding sovereignty over Bajo de Masinloc and the Kalayaan Island Group.

  • The UK is steadfast in its support of UNAMA's work in Afghanistan: UK Explanation of Vote at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 16 Mar 2026 -- Explanation of Vote by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Afghanistan.
  • Security Council Extends United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan, Unanimously Adopting Resolution 2818 (2026) UN Security Council 16 Mar 2026 -- The Security Council today decided to extend the United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan (UNAMA) until 17 June, as speakers urged the Taliban to reverse its policy banning Afghan women from UN buildings and underscored the Mission's critical role in supporting the Afghan people amid continuing humanitarian, security and human rights challenges.

  • China to continue to make active efforts to facilitate reconciliation and ease tensions between Afghanistan, Pakistan: FM Global Times 16 Mar 2026 -- China will continue to make active efforts to facilitate reconciliation and ease tensions between the two sides, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Monday. China hopes that both sides will remain calm and exercise restraint, engage face to face as soon as possible, achieve a ceasefire at the earliest opportunity, and resolve differences and disputes through dialogue, he added.

  • 'Hope' for Haiti's political future as some 300 groups reportedly register for upcoming elections UN News 16 Mar 2026 -- Haiti remains mired in a multidimensional crisis marked by weak institutions, political uncertainty, widespread gang violence and overwhelming humanitarian needs, but a recent new agreement by political groups offers "a moment of hope and progress for the Haitian people", according to the UN's most senior official in the Caribbean country.
  • 'Glimmer of hope' in Haiti amid shifting gang frontlines UN News 16 Mar 2026 -- The liberation of territory from gangs and a more "motivated and visible" police presence has provided a "glimmer of hope" for Haiti as the Caribbean island nation continues to struggle with violence, insecurity and poverty.

News Reports

  • Council adopts conclusions on advancing the EU's capacity to counter hybrid threats EC/CEU 16 Mar 2026 -- The Council today approved conclusions on advancing the EU's capacity to counter hybrid threats, reaffirming the EU's determination to use all available tools to prevent, deter and respond to hybrid campaigns targeting the Union, its member states, and its partners irrespective of their origin, scale and intensity.

  • Cyber-attacks against the EU and its member states: Council sanctions three entities and two individuals EC/CEU 16 Mar 2026 -- The Council adopted today restrictive measures against three entities and two individuals responsible for cyber-attacks carried out against EU member states and EU partners.

  • Meeting with Head of Udmurtia Alexander Brechalov President of Russia 16 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a working meeting with Head of the Udmurt Republic Alexander Brechalov.
  • Telephone conversation with Prime Minister of Ethiopia Abiy Ahmed President of Russia 16 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with Prime Minister of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia Abiy Ahmed.
  • Telephone conversation with Prime Minister of Ethiopia Abiy Ahmed President of Russia 16 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with Prime Minister of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia Abiy Ahmed.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's talks with Musalia Mudavadi, Prime Cabinet Secretary and Cabinet Secretary for Foreign and Diaspora Affairs of Kenya Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Mar 2026
  • Russian hybrid threats: four individuals added to EU sanctions list for information manipulation activities EC/CEU 16 Mar 2026 -- The Council today decided to adopt restrictive measures against an additional four individuals responsible for Russia's continued hybrid activities, in particular Foreign Information Manipulation and Interference (FIMI)against the EU and its member states and partners.
  • Tsahkna at EU foreign ministers' meeting: Russia must not benefit from developments in the Middle East Estonia MFA 16 Mar 2026 -- At the meeting of European Union foreign ministers in Brussels today, 16 March, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna stressed that Russia must not be allowed to benefit from rising global oil prices caused by hostilities in the Middle East.
  • Baiba Braže at the EU Foreign Affairs Council: Russia is our common problem, and we must continue to contain it - through new sanctions, by restricting its economy, and by preventing the circumvention of sanctions Latvia MFA 16 Mar 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on March 16, 2026 PRC MofA 16 Mar 2026
  • China, US maintain communication regarding Trump's China visit: FM Global Times 16 Mar 2026
  • China releases full text of NPC Standing Committee work report People's Daily 16 Mar 2026 -- The full text of the report on the work of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress (NPC) was released via Xinhua News Agency on Sunday.
  • China launches new remote sensing satellite Global Times 16 Mar 2026 -- China successfully launched a remote sensing satellite from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in north China's Shanxi Province on Sunday.
  • China's Kuaizhou-11 rocket completes 5th commercial flight, places 8 payloads into orbit Global Times 16 Mar 2026 -- China on Monday launched the Kuaizhou-11 Y7 carrier rocket from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center in Northwest China's Gansu Province, sending eight new satellites into preset orbits, the Xinhua News Agency reported.
  • China launches new remote sensing satellite People's Daily 16 Mar 2026 -- China successfully launched a remote sensing satellite from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in north China's Shanxi Province on Sunday.

  • Mainland slams Lai Ching-te's secessionist remarks People's Daily 16 Mar 2026 -- The Chinese mainland on Saturday condemned Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te for making secessionist remarks advocating "Taiwan independence" and challenging the one-China principle.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 16 Mar 2026

  • Senior Party and Government Officials Vote in Election of SPA Deputies KCNA 16 Mar 2026 -- Senior Party and government officials went to sub-constituencies in different parts of the DPRK to take part in the election of deputies to the 15th Supreme People's Assembly together with voters on March 15.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Votes in Election of SPA Deputies and Encourages Workers at Chonsong Youth Coal Mine KCNA 16 Mar 2026 -- The election of deputies to the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK took place at all the constituencies across the country on March 15 when the whole country was overflowing with the people's joy and excitement of greeting the historic day which will serve as an occasion to further consolidate the people-centered socialist system of Korean style and provide a powerful sovereign guarantee for the comprehensive development of the state.
  • Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech to Workers and Officials of Chonsong Youth Coal Mine KCNA 16 Mar 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un voted in the election of deputies to the Supreme People's Assembly at the Chonsong Youth Coal Mine and made a speech to the workers and officials of the coal mine.
  • Report of Central Election Committee KCNA 16 Mar 2026 -- The central election committee for the election of deputies to the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea issued the following report on March 15:
  • Concrete steps and international cooperation key to human rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea: UN expert OHCHR 16 Mar 2026 -- Concrete steps and international cooperation are key to improving human rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), the UN Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, Elizabeth Salmón, said today.
  • DPRK: Statement by the Spokesperson on the launch of ballistic missiles European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Mar 2026 -- The launch of ballistic missiles on 14 March represents further violations of UN Security Council resolutions.
  • Japan-U.S.-ROK Foreign Ministries' Official Telephone Meeting on North Korea Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 16 Mar 2026
  • FCDO statement on DPRK ballistic missile launches: 14 March UK FCDO 16 Mar 2026 -- The FCDO has released a statement following ballistic missile launches by the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) on 14 March.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Interactive Dialogue on the Democratic People's Republic of Korea UK FCDO 16 Mar 2026 -- UK Statement for the Interactive Dialogue with the Special Rapporteur on the Democratic People's Republic of Korea. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

  • Iran: Council sanctions an additional 16 persons and three entities over serious human rights violations EC/CEU 16 Mar 2026 -- The Council decided today to impose restrictive measures on an additional 16 persons and three entities responsible for serious human rights violations in Iran.
  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Joint Interactive Dialogue with the Special Rapporteur on human rights and the Independent and International Fact Finding Mission in Iran European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Mar 2026
  • UN expert warns of deepening human rights crisis in Iran OHCHR 16 Mar 2026 -- As military tensions escalate in the region, Iranian authorities must immediately end their excessive use of force against civilians, release all those detained for exercising their fundamental freedoms, restore unhindered internet access and allow civil society to carry out its vital human rights work, a UN expert said today.
  • IRGC arrests five Mossad-linked agents in northwest Iran Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- The Intelligence Organization of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) in the northwestern Iranian province of West Azarbaijan says it has arrested five agents for sharing information with the Israeli-backed terrorist "Iran International" television channel.
  • Iran arrests 500 individuals over spying for enemies: Police chief Press TV 16 Mar 2026 -- Iran's police chief, Brigadier General Ahmad Reza Radan, says that country's police forces have arrested 500 people for sharing information with the enemies amid the ongoing US-Israel war.

  • Joint Statement - Minister's Barrot's visit to the Central African Republic (March 16, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 16 Mar 2026 -- Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Jean-Noël Barrot paid a working visit to the Central African city of Bangui on March 12 and 13.

  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Carney of Canada: 16 March 2026 Gov.UK 16 Mar 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Prime Minister of the United Kingdom Sir Keir Starmer Prime Minister of Canada 16 Mar 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, met with the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (UK), Sir Keir Starmer, during his bilateral visit to the UK.

  • Japan-U.S. Foreign Ministers' Telephone Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 16 Mar 2026
  • Japan and the EU hold the 6th meeting of the Joint Committee of the Japan-EU Strategic Partnership Agreement (SPA) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 16 Mar 2026
  • EU-Japan: 6th meeting of the Joint Committee of the EU-Japan Strategic Partnership Agreement (SPA) held in Tokyo European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Mar 2026 -- On 16 March, the 6th meeting of the Joint Committee under the EU-Japan Strategic Partnership Agreement (SPA) was held in Tokyo. It was co-chaired by Mr. Kitagawa Katsuro, Director-General of the European Affairs Bureau, Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan, and Mr. Erik Kurzweil, Managing Director for Asia and the Pacific, European External Action Service.

  • Kazakh Voters Appear To Approve Controversial Changes That Critics Say May Concentrate President's Power RFE/RL 16 Mar 2026 -- Kazakh voters appear to have approved a controversial new constitution in a March 15 referendum that critics say could further concentrate power in the presidency.

  • Foreign Affairs Council: Press conference by High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Affairs Council: Press remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas upon arrival European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Mar 202

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive Dialogue with the Group of Human Rights Experts on Nicaragua European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Mar 2026

  • World News in Brief: Deadly drone strikes in Sudan, families killed in West Bank, Ukraine casualties update UN News 16 Mar 2026 -- Relentless drone strikes across Sudan's Kordofan and Darfur regions are causing civilian casualties and further restricting humanitarian access, the UN warned on Monday.
